critical_blockers.check: Source code is free of removed, deprecated-for-removal, and internal JDK APIs for the selected target
critical_blockers.action: Replace unsupported JDK APIs before changing the runtime

critical_blockers.check: Remote calls use deadlines, bounded retries, failure isolation, and operation-safe idempotency
critical_blockers.action: Set connect and response deadlines and retry only safe transient failures

critical_blockers.check: Request, response, and event changes preserve backward compatibility
critical_blockers.action: Prefer additive evolution and retain compatibility during the migration window

priority_actions.check: Request, response, and event changes preserve backward compatibility
priority_actions.action: Prefer additive evolution and retain compatibility during the migration window

priority_actions.check: Remote calls use deadlines, bounded retries, failure isolation, and operation-safe idempotency
priority_actions.action: Set connect and response deadlines and retry only safe transient failures

priority_actions.check: Source code is free of removed, deprecated-for-removal, and internal JDK APIs for the selected target
priority_actions.action: Replace unsupported JDK APIs before changing the runtime

priority_actions.check: Package or module boundaries and dependency direction are explicit and enforceable
priority_actions.action: Define boundaries and enforce them with architecture tests

priority_actions.check: Database migrations are versioned, additive, and backward-compatible
priority_actions.action: Separate additive and destructive changes across a compatibility window

priority_actions.check: Java and Spring Boot versions are explicit and compatible with the selected supported target
priority_actions.action: Select a supported target pair and make compiler and runtime versions explicit

priority_actions.check: Authentication and authorization protect every exposed boundary
priority_actions.action: Enforce endpoint and method authorization with deny-by-default behavior

priority_actions.check: Critical business behavior has automated regression coverage
priority_actions.action: Protect current behavior before refactoring or upgrading

priority_actions.check: Business logic is separated from web, persistence, messaging, and framework-specific code
priority_actions.action: Move business decisions behind framework-neutral application or domain interfaces

priority_actions.check: Method, class, and module complexity is controlled
priority_actions.action: Split high-complexity code along cohesive responsibilities

priority_actions.check: Async tasks, schedulers, executors, and mutable shared state are safe and bounded
priority_actions.action: Use bounded executors and remove unsafe shared mutable state

priority_actions.check: API and message compatibility is verified automatically
priority_actions.action: Run compatibility tests before changing public contracts

priority_actions.check: Namespaces, annotations, bytecode tools, and library integrations are compatible with the target stack
priority_actions.action: Upgrade or replace incompatible integrations before the framework change

priority_actions.check: HTTP APIs and message schemas have machine-readable contracts
priority_actions.action: Version contract artifacts with the code that implements them

priority_actions.check: Versioning, status codes, error models, and deprecation behavior are consistent
priority_actions.action: Standardize public error semantics and make deprecation explicit

priority_actions.check: External systems are accessed through stable interfaces or adapters
priority_actions.action: Isolate external clients so integrations can be upgraded and tested independently

priority_actions.check: Entity mapping, fetch strategy, cascade behavior, locking, and schema generation are explicit
priority_actions.action: Remove risky implicit ORM behavior and document intentional mappings

priority_actions.check: Transaction boundaries, propagation, isolation, rollback rules, and timeouts are explicit
priority_actions.action: Place transaction boundaries in the service layer and configure non-default semantics deliberately

priority_actions.check: Duplication, dead code, and unused dependencies are minimized
priority_actions.action: Remove unused paths and consolidate duplicated behavior before migration

priority_actions.check: Logs are structured, correlated, and exclude sensitive data
priority_actions.action: Use structured events and propagate correlation context across boundaries

priority_actions.check: Metrics instrument requests, dependencies, messaging, and executors
priority_actions.action: Expose traffic, error, latency, and saturation signals at code boundaries

priority_actions.check: Health, readiness, and startup state reflect application and dependency conditions
priority_actions.action: Report readiness only after required application state is usable

priority_actions.check: Exceptions are classified and are not swallowed or converted into false success
priority_actions.action: Preserve failure semantics and remove catch-all success responses

priority_actions.check: Message consumers handle duplicates, poison messages, dead letters, and replay
priority_actions.action: Make handlers idempotent and define bounded recovery paths

priority_actions.check: Dependency and build-plugin versions are controlled, convergent, and inventory-ready
priority_actions.action: Centralize versions, remove duplicate overrides, and resolve dependency convergence failures

priority_actions.check: Dependencies and build plugins can be checked for vulnerabilities and supply-chain risk
priority_actions.action: Automate component analysis and review actionable findings

priority_actions.check: Source and configuration do not rely on deprecated Spring APIs, mechanisms, or properties for the selected target
priority_actions.action: Apply each skipped migration guide and remove temporary migration helpers

priority_actions.check: Serialization rules for dates, enums, nulls, and unknown fields are explicit
priority_actions.action: Make wire-format behavior explicit and tolerant where compatibility requires it

priority_actions.check: Circular dependencies, service locators, static application-context access, and hidden framework coupling are absent
priority_actions.action: Replace hidden coupling with constructor-injected explicit dependencies

priority_actions.check: Queries, pagination, batching, and bulk operations have explicit bounds
priority_actions.action: Add pagination or batching and prevent unbounded data loading

priority_actions.check: Trace context propagates across HTTP, messaging, and asynchronous boundaries
priority_actions.action: Preserve trace context across every asynchronous or remote boundary

priority_actions.check: Framework extension points and default-sensitive behavior are inventoried as migration hotspots
priority_actions.action: Review each extension point against target release notes and make critical defaults explicit

priority_actions.check: Technical-debt markers and suppressions are scoped and actionable
priority_actions.action: Replace broad suppressions and convert migration debt into explicit actions

priority_actions.check: Static-analysis rules are versioned and broad suppressions are avoided
priority_actions.action: Adopt enforceable rules and keep exclusions narrow and documented

priority_actions.check: JVM, container, executor, thread-pool, and connection-pool settings are explicit
priority_actions.action: Declare resource settings and avoid environment-dependent implicit defaults

priority_actions.check: Tests are deterministic, isolated, and independent of execution order
priority_actions.action: Remove shared state, timing assumptions, and environment-dependent test behavior
